sections.title: SECTION 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best answer. Then on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Listen to an example.

On the recording, you hear:

(narrator)
(man) That exam was just awful. 
(woman) Oh, it could have been worse. 
(narrator) What does the woman mean? 

In your test book, you read:
(A) The exam was really awful. 
(B) It was the worst exam she had ever seen. 
(C) It couldn't have been more difficult. 
(D) It wasn't that hard. 

You learn from the conversation that the man thought the exam was very difficult and that the woman disagreed with the man. The best answer to the question, "What does the woman mean?" is (D), "It wasn't that hard." Therefore, the correct choice is (D).

questions.passage:
W: Are you enjoying your coffee?
M: It tastes extremely bitter this morning!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The coffee is much better this morning.

reason.en: The man says the coffee tastes 'extremely bitter,' which is the opposite of 'much better.'
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan kopi terasa 'sangat pahit,' yang merupakan kebalikan dari 'jauh lebih baik.'

reason.en: The man describes the coffee as 'extremely bitter,' not 'extremely good.'
reason.id: Pria itu menggambarkan kopi sebagai 'sangat pahit,' bukan 'sangat enak.'

reason.en: The man's statement 'It tastes extremely bitter this morning!' implies that the coffee is not good. 'Bitter' is a negative descriptor for coffee, and the answer choice 'The coffee isn't very good' paraphrases this meaning using synonyms.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'Rasanya sangat pahit pagi ini!' menyiratkan bahwa kopi itu tidak enak. 'Pahit' adalah deskriptor negatif untuk kopi, dan pilihan jawaban 'Kopi itu tidak terlalu enak' memparafrasekan makna ini menggunakan sinonim.

options.text: This morning he definitely wants some coffee.

reason.en: The man's comment about the taste of the coffee does not indicate his desire for coffee this morning.
reason.id: Komentar pria itu tentang rasa kopi tidak menunjukkan keinginannya untuk minum kopi pagi ini.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Can you tell me how often the philosophy class meets?
M: It meets twice a week, for an hour and a half each time.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The two classes meet in an hour and a half.

reason.en: The dialogue states the class meets twice a week for an hour and a half each time, not that the two classes meet in an hour and a half.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan kelas bertemu dua kali seminggu selama satu setengah jam setiap kali, bukan bahwa kedua kelas bertemu dalam satu setengah jam.

options.text: The class meets three hours per week.

reason.en: The man states the class meets twice a week for an hour and a half each time. Two times 1.5 hours equals 3 hours per week. This answer paraphrases the information given.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan kelas bertemu dua kali seminggu selama satu setengah jam setiap kali. Dua kali 1,5 jam sama dengan 3 jam per minggu. Jawaban ini memparafrasekan informasi yang diberikan.

options.text: Each half of the class is an hour long.

reason.en: The dialogue states the class meets for an hour and a half each time, not that each half of the class is an hour long.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan kelas bertemu selama satu setengah jam setiap kali, bukan bahwa setiap setengah dari kelas berdurasi satu jam.

options.text: Two times a week the class meets for an hour.

reason.en: The dialogue states the class meets for an hour and a half each time, not for one hour.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan kelas bertemu selama satu setengah jam setiap kali, bukan selama satu jam.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
I'm tired of just sitting here!
M: W: Relax. I'm sure that the flight will depart within a few minutes.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: A few minutes ago, the flight departed.

reason.en: The woman says the flight WILL depart, not that it already departed. This option contradicts the woman's statement.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut mengatakan penerbangan AKAN berangkat, bukan bahwa penerbangan tersebut sudah berangkat. Pilihan ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita tersebut.

options.text: The fight will start in a while.

reason.en: The woman says 'within a few minutes,' which implies a short but definite period of time. 'In a while' is more vague and less certain than 'within a few minutes.' The phrase 'depart' is also more specific than 'start.'
reason.id: Wanita tersebut mengatakan 'dalam beberapa menit', yang menyiratkan periode waktu yang singkat namun pasti. 'Nanti' lebih tidak jelas dan kurang pasti daripada 'dalam beberapa menit'. Frasa 'berangkat' juga lebih spesifik daripada 'mulai'.

options.text: They are frightened about the departure.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention fear or being frightened. The word 'tired' from the man is about impatience, not fear.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan rasa takut atau ketakutan. Kata 'lelah' dari pria tersebut berkaitan dengan ketidaksabaran, bukan ketakutan.

options.text: The plane is going to take off soon.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'I'm sure that the flight will depart within a few minutes' directly implies that the plane is going to take off soon. 'Depart' in the context of a flight means to take off.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita tersebut 'Saya yakin penerbangan akan berangkat dalam beberapa menit' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa pesawat akan lepas landas segera. 'Berangkat' dalam konteks penerbangan berarti lepas landas.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
W: The science project is due next week.
M: I suppose I'll have to start working on it now.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He hasn't yet begun his project.

reason.en: The man says, 'I suppose I'll have to start working on it now.' This implies that he has not yet begun his project and needs to start immediately because it is due next week. This aligns with the meaning of 'start working on it now'.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata, 'Saya kira saya harus mulai mengerjakannya sekarang.' Ini menyiratkan bahwa dia belum memulai proyeknya dan perlu segera memulainya karena tenggat waktunya minggu depan. Ini sejalan dengan arti 'mulai mengerjakannya sekarang'.

options.text: He's supposed to do his science project next week.

reason.en: The woman states the project is due next week, not that the man is supposed to do it next week. The man's response indicates he needs to start working on it, not that the due date is next week.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyatakan proyek tersebut jatuh tempo minggu depan, bukan bahwa pria itu seharusnya mengerjakannya minggu depan. Tanggapan pria itu menunjukkan bahwa dia perlu mulai mengerjakannya, bukan bahwa tenggat waktunya adalah minggu depan.

options.text: He needs to start working on changing the due date.

reason.en: There is no mention of changing the due date in the dialogue. The man's statement is about starting the project itself.
reason.id: Tidak ada penyebutan tentang mengubah tanggal jatuh tempo dalam dialog. Pernyataan pria itu adalah tentang memulai proyek itu sendiri.

options.text: He's been working steadily on his science project.

reason.en: The man's statement, 'I suppose I'll have to start working on it now,' directly contradicts the idea that he has been working steadily. It suggests he is just beginning.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu, 'Saya kira saya harus mulai mengerjakannya sekarang,' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia telah bekerja terus-menerus. Ini menunjukkan bahwa dia baru saja memulai.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: I'd like to order a dozen roses. Do you deliver?
W: Yes. We can deliver anywhere in the city by this afternoon.
questions.question: 5. WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSATION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

reason.en: The conversation is about ordering flowers for delivery, not about sending or receiving mail.
reason.id: Percakapan ini tentang memesan bunga untuk pengiriman, bukan tentang mengirim atau menerima surat.

reason.en: The man is ordering a dozen roses and asking about delivery, which are services typically offered by a florist shop.
reason.id: Pria itu memesan selusin mawar dan bertanya tentang pengiriman, yang merupakan layanan yang biasanya ditawarkan oleh toko bunga.

reason.en: The conversation is about ordering flowers, not about dining or food service.
reason.id: Percakapan ini tentang memesan bunga, bukan tentang makan atau layanan makanan.

reason.en: The conversation is about ordering flowers for delivery, not about a hospital setting.
reason.id: Percakapan ini tentang memesan bunga untuk pengiriman, bukan tentang suasana rumah sakit.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
W: Did you enjoy the biology lecture?
M: The professor droned on and on about cell division.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The professor drowned the cells in a lab.

reason.en: The word 'droned on and on' means to speak in a monotonous, boring way. It does not mean to physically drown something. The word 'drowned' is a homophone for 'droned' and is used as a distractor.
reason.id: Kata 'droned on and on' berarti berbicara dengan cara yang monoton dan membosankan. Itu tidak berarti menenggelamkan sesuatu secara fisik. Kata 'drowned' adalah homofon dari 'droned' dan digunakan sebagai pengalih perhatian.

options.text: The lecture was long and boring.

reason.en: The man's statement 'The professor droned on and on about cell division' directly implies that the lecture was long and boring. 'Droned on and on' is an idiom for speaking monotonously and at length.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'The professor droned on and on about cell division' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa kuliah itu panjang dan membosankan. 'Droned on and on' adalah idiom untuk berbicara secara monoton dan dalam waktu lama.

options.text: The professor divided the lecture into parts.

reason.en: The phrase 'droned on and on' refers to the manner of speaking, not the structure of the lecture. There is no information to suggest the lecture was divided into parts.
reason.id: Frasa 'droned on and on' mengacu pada cara berbicara, bukan struktur kuliah. Tidak ada informasi yang menunjukkan kuliah dibagi menjadi beberapa bagian.

options.text: The biologist tried to sell the results of the experiment.

reason.en: The dialogue is about a biology lecture on cell division. There is no mention of selling experiment results or any biologist trying to sell anything.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang kuliah biologi tentang pembelahan sel. Tidak ada penyebutan tentang penjualan hasil eksperimen atau ahli biologi yang mencoba menjual sesuatu.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: What do I need to cash a check?
W: I have to see a driver's license and a credit card.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She needs to get a driver's license.

reason.en: The woman is stating what she needs from the man, not what she needs to obtain.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyatakan apa yang dia butuhkan dari pria itu, bukan apa yang dia butuhkan untuk diperoleh.

options.text: Two pieces of identification are necessary.

reason.en: The woman lists two specific items (driver's license and credit card) that are required, implying that two forms of identification are necessary.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyebutkan dua item spesifik (SIM dan kartu kredit) yang diperlukan, menyiratkan bahwa dua bentuk identifikasi diperlukan.

options.text: The man should check to see if he needs credit.

reason.en: The woman is stating a requirement, not advising the man to check his credit.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyatakan persyaratan, bukan menyarankan pria itu untuk memeriksa kreditnya.

options.text: A credit card can be used to get a driver's license.

reason.en: The dialogue does not suggest that a credit card can be used to obtain a driver's license; they are presented as separate requirements.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyarankan bahwa kartu kredit dapat digunakan untuk mendapatkan SIM; keduanya disajikan sebagai persyaratan terpisah.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
W: Have you been able to find an apartment yet?
M: It's difficult to find affordable housing in New York.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Housing within his budget is hard to locate.

reason.en: The man states that finding 'affordable housing' is difficult. Option 0 paraphrases this by saying 'Housing within his budget is hard to locate,' using synonyms like 'affordable' for 'within his budget' and 'locate' for 'find'. This aligns with Skill 2, which emphasizes choosing answers with synonyms.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa menemukan 'perumahan yang terjangkau' itu sulit. Pilihan 0 memparafrasekan ini dengan mengatakan 'Perumahan dalam anggarannya sulit ditemukan', menggunakan sinonim seperti 'terjangkau' untuk 'dalam anggarannya' dan 'ditemukan' untuk 'mencari'. Ini sejalan dengan Skill 2, yang menekankan memilih jawaban dengan sinonim.

options.text: It's hard to find his house in New York.

reason.en: The dialogue is about finding an apartment, not locating his house. The word 'house' is used incorrectly here, and the focus is on affordability, not just location.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang mencari apartemen, bukan menemukan rumahnya. Kata 'rumah' digunakan di sini secara keliru, dan fokusnya adalah pada keterjangkauan, bukan hanya lokasi.

options.text: He can't afford to move his house to New York.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention moving his house. The man is looking for an apartment to rent or buy, and the issue is affordability, not the act of moving a physical house.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan pemindahan rumahnya. Pria itu sedang mencari apartemen untuk disewa atau dibeli, dan masalahnya adalah keterjangkauan, bukan tindakan memindahkan rumah fisik.

options.text: Housing in New York is unavailable.

reason.en: The man says it's difficult to find affordable housing, not that housing is unavailable. There might be housing available, but it's too expensive for him.
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan sulit menemukan perumahan yang terjangkau, bukan bahwa perumahan tidak tersedia. Mungkin ada perumahan yang tersedia, tetapi terlalu mahal baginya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Why were you so late in getting home from work?
M: My boss had me finish all the month-end reports.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The boss was working on the reports.

reason.en: The man's boss assigned him the task, not that the boss was doing the reports himself.
reason.id: Bos pria itu menugaskannya tugas itu, bukan bahwa bos itu sendiri yang mengerjakan laporan itu.

options.text: He would have to finish the reports before the end of next month.

reason.en: The dialogue refers to 'month-end reports,' implying they are for the current month's end, not a future deadline.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut merujuk pada 'laporan akhir bulan', menyiratkan bahwa laporan tersebut untuk akhir bulan ini, bukan tenggat waktu di masa depan.

options.text: He was directed to stay late and finish some work.

reason.en: The phrase 'My boss had me finish...' is a passive construction indicating the man was instructed or required by his boss to complete the reports, causing him to be late.
reason.id: Frasa 'My boss had me finish...' adalah konstruksi pasif yang menunjukkan bahwa pria itu diinstruksikan atau diwajibkan oleh bosnya untuk menyelesaikan laporan, yang membuatnya terlambat.

options.text: He could finish the reports at home.

reason.en: The dialogue implies the work was done at the office, leading to his lateness in getting home, not that he took the work home.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut menyiratkan bahwa pekerjaan itu dilakukan di kantor, yang menyebabkan dia terlambat pulang, bukan bahwa dia membawa pekerjaan itu pulang.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 5: Listen for Who and What in Passives

questions.passage:
M: Ms. Jones did not look too happy as she left her classroom.
W: She was angered by her rowdy students.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The boisterous students made the teacher mad.

reason.en: The dialogue states that Ms. Jones 'did not look too happy' and 'was angered by her rowdy students.' Option [0] paraphrases this by saying 'The boisterous students made the teacher mad.' 'Boisterous' is a synonym for 'rowdy,' and 'mad' is a synonym for 'angered.' This aligns with Skill 2, which emphasizes choosing answers with synonyms.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan bahwa Ms. Jones 'tidak terlihat terlalu senang' dan 'marah karena murid-muridnya yang berisik.' Pilihan [0] memparafrasekan ini dengan mengatakan 'Murid-murid yang berisik membuat guru marah.' 'Berisik' adalah sinonim untuk 'rowdy,' dan 'marah' adalah sinonim untuk 'angered.' Ini sejalan dengan Skill 2, yang menekankan memilih jawaban dengan sinonim.

options.text: The teacher angered the students with the exam results.

reason.en: The dialogue states the teacher was angered by the students, not the other way around. Option [1] reverses the cause and effect and uses 'exam results,' which is not mentioned.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan bahwa guru marah karena murid-murid, bukan sebaliknya. Pilihan [1] membalikkan sebab dan akibat dan menggunakan 'hasil ujian,' yang tidak disebutkan.

options.text: The students were angry that the teacher was around.

reason.en: The dialogue states the students angered the teacher, not that the students were angry that the teacher was around. Option [2] misinterprets the direction of the anger.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan bahwa murid-murid membuat guru marah, bukan bahwa murid-murid marah karena guru ada di sekitar. Pilihan [2] salah menafsirkan arah kemarahan.

options.text: The angered students complained to the teacher.

reason.en: The dialogue states the teacher was angered by the students. Option [3] suggests the students were complaining, which is not stated, and reverses the roles of who was angry.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan bahwa guru marah karena murid-murid. Pilihan [3] menyarankan bahwa murid-murid mengeluh, yang tidak dinyatakan, dan membalikkan peran siapa yang marah.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: The prices at this store are really outrageous!
M: You can say that again!
questions.question: 11. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

reason.en: The man's statement 'You can say that again!' is an expression of strong agreement, meaning he does not think the prices are reasonable.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'You can say that again!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan yang kuat, yang berarti dia tidak berpikir harga tersebut masuk akal.

options.text: The store is too far out of town.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the prices being too high, not the store's location.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas tentang harga yang terlalu tinggi, bukan lokasi toko.

options.text: He would like the woman to repeat what she said.

reason.en: The phrase 'You can say that again!' is an idiom for agreement, not a request for repetition.
reason.id: Frasa 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom untuk persetujuan, bukan permintaan untuk mengulang.

reason.en: The man's phrase 'You can say that again!' is a common idiom used to express strong agreement with what the previous speaker said.
reason.id: Frasa pria 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom umum yang digunakan untuk menyatakan persetujuan kuat dengan apa yang dikatakan pembicara sebelumnya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
W: I don't like this weather very much.
M: We haven't see rain like this for many years!
questions.question: 12. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: It's rained unusually hard this year.

reason.en: The man's statement 'We haven't seen rain like this for many years!' implies that the current rain is unusual and has not occurred for a long time, meaning it has rained unusually hard this year. This aligns with the meaning of the negative expression.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'We haven't seen rain like this for many years!' menyiratkan bahwa hujan saat ini tidak biasa dan belum terjadi untuk waktu yang lama, yang berarti tahun ini hujannya sangat deras. Ini sejalan dengan makna ekspresi negatif.

options.text: There hasn't been any rain for many years.

reason.en: The man says they 'haven't seen rain like this for many years,' which means they have seen rain, but not like this. This option incorrectly states there hasn't been any rain at all.
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan bahwa mereka 'belum melihat hujan seperti ini selama bertahun-tahun,' yang berarti mereka telah melihat hujan, tetapi tidak seperti ini. Pilihan ini secara keliru menyatakan bahwa sama sekali tidak ada hujan.

options.text: It's been many years since it rained.

reason.en: The man's statement implies that it has rained, but not with this intensity, for many years. This option suggests that it has been many years since any rain occurred, which is a misinterpretation of the negative expression.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu menyiratkan bahwa sudah bertahun-tahun hujan turun, tetapi tidak dengan intensitas seperti ini. Pilihan ini menyarankan bahwa sudah bertahun-tahun sejak hujan turun, yang merupakan salah tafsir dari ekspresi negatif.

reason.en: The man's statement is about the unusual amount of rain, not his personal preference for rain. The dialogue does not provide information about whether he likes or dislikes rain.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu adalah tentang jumlah hujan yang tidak biasa, bukan preferensi pribadinya terhadap hujan. Dialog tersebut tidak memberikan informasi apakah dia suka atau tidak suka hujan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
M: Professor Martin, what do you think of the composition that I turned in last week?
W: Without question, you need to improve the quality of your writing.
questions.question: 13. WHAT DOES PROFESSOR MARTIN SAY ABOUT THE STUDENT?

options.text: He needs to do a better job writing questions.

reason.en: The student turned in a composition, not questions. The professor's feedback is about the quality of writing, not question formulation.
reason.id: Mahasiswa menyerahkan komposisi, bukan pertanyaan. Umpan balik profesor adalah tentang kualitas tulisan, bukan perumusan pertanyaan.

options.text: He certainly must make his writing better.

reason.en: The professor states, 'Without question, you need to improve the quality of your writing.' This is paraphrased in option [1] as 'He certainly must make his writing better.' 'Improve the quality' is synonymous with 'make better,' and 'without question' is synonymous with 'certainly.' This aligns with Skill 2, which emphasizes choosing answers with synonyms.
reason.id: Profesor menyatakan, 'Tanpa ragu, Anda perlu meningkatkan kualitas tulisan Anda.' Ini diparafrasekan dalam opsi [1] sebagai 'Dia pasti harus membuat tulisannya lebih baik.' 'Meningkatkan kualitas' sinonim dengan 'membuat lebih baik,' dan 'tanpa ragu' sinonim dengan 'pasti.' Ini selaras dengan Skill 2, yang menekankan memilih jawaban dengan sinonim.

options.text: Without the questions, he cannot write the answers.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the student's composition, not about questions and answers. This option introduces irrelevant concepts.
reason.id: Dialognya tentang komposisi mahasiswa, bukan tentang pertanyaan dan jawaban. Opsi ini memperkenalkan konsep yang tidak relevan.

options.text: He needs to understand the written questions better.

reason.en: The professor's comment is about the quality of the student's writing, not about understanding written questions. This option misinterprets the context.
reason.id: Komentar profesor adalah tentang kualitas tulisan mahasiswa, bukan tentang memahami pertanyaan tertulis. Opsi ini salah menafsirkan konteks.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Where should I go next?
M: You must stand in this line so that the agent can check your passport.
(D: I He can check her passport instead of the agent.
questions.question: 14. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The agent was standing in line with his passport.

reason.en: The man's statement implies that the woman needs to wait her turn. Option 0 misinterprets the roles and actions.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria menyiratkan bahwa wanita perlu menunggu gilirannya. Opsi 0 salah menafsirkan peran dan tindakan.

options.text: The line to get new passports is very long.

reason.en: The dialogue is about checking a passport, not about the length of a line for new passports. Option 1 introduces irrelevant information.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang pemeriksaan paspor, bukan tentang panjang antrean untuk paspor baru. Opsi 1 memperkenalkan informasi yang tidak relevan.

options.text: The woman must wait her turn to get her passport checked.

reason.en: The man's instruction, 'You must stand in this line so that the agent can check your passport,' implies that the woman must wait her turn in the line. This aligns with Skill 7, which deals with understanding negative implications or requirements, even when not explicitly stated as negative.
reason.id: Instruksi pria, 'Anda harus berdiri di antrean ini agar agen dapat memeriksa paspor Anda,' menyiratkan bahwa wanita harus menunggu gilirannya di antrean. groups.passage:
N: Listen to a conversation on a university campus. 

M: You seem to know your way around campus. Have you been here long? 
w: I'm a senior literature major. I'll be graduating next June. 
M: Your major is literature? Mine is, too.But I'm just beginning my work in my major. I just transferred to this university from a junior college. Perhaps you could tell me about the courses you've got to take for a literature major. 
w: Well, for a literature major you need to take eight courses, three required courses and five electives. First, you have to take "Survey of World Literature, Parts One and Two." This is really two courses, and it'll take two semesters, and it's required for all literature majors. The other course required for all literature majors is "Introduction to Literary Analysis." 
M: You mean, if I want to specialize in American literature, I still must take two semesters of World literature?  
w: Yes, because the two semesters are required for all literature majors. 
M: But I only want to study American literature! 
w: At least you can take all of your five elective courses in the area that you want. 
M: That's what I'll do, then.
groups.label: Q 31-34

groups.passage:
Listen to a conversation between two friends. 
w: Wasn't that a fascinating lecture on dolphins? I didn't know that dolphins traveled in such large groups, or were able to communicate with other members of their group with those whistlelike sounds. 
M: And they also use clicks as a sort of sonar. 
w: I really couldn't understand that part of the lecture. You could? 
M: Yes, the dolphins use clicks to identify objects in the water; they can even identify tiny objects more than 100 meters away using these clicks. Scientists believe that a dolphin may even have a sonar-like image in its brain of a distant object so that it can identify the object long before the dolphin can actually see the object. 
w: So the dolphins use these clicks mostly to identify objects in the water? 
M: I think so, and they have considerably more ability to do this than humans do. 
w: It is hard to believe that, in addition to these sonar clicks, dolphins are actually learning some human language. 
M: Yes, I believe that the lecturer said that some dolphins had already learned around fifty human commands, and that those dolphins were able to understand not only individual words but words clustered together in sentences! 
w: Dolphins must certainly be amazing animals to do all of that. 
M:  I'm sure they are, and we're only just beginning to find out how intelligent they are.

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to a welcome address by a member of a club. 
(woman) Welcome to this introductory meeting for new members of the Sierra Club. The Sierra Club is an organization whose goals are centered on the protection of the environment. It was founded in 1892 in San Francisco by naturalist John Muir, who was intent on preserving the natural beauty and harmony of the Sierra Nevadas in eastern California. 

Today the Sierra Club boasts almost 200,000 members in all fifty states of the United States. Through activities such as conferences, lectures, exhibits, and films, the organization works to continue the effort begun by John Muir. The Sierra Club also publishes a weekly newsletter, a bimonthly magazine, and various books.

On the right you can see the university stadium, which is used for football and soccer as well as track and field. sections.instruction:
Time—approximately 35 minutes 
(including the reading of the directions for each part) 

In this section of the test, you will have an opportunity to demonstrate your ability to understand conversations and talks in English. There are three parts to this section. Answer all the questions on the basis of what is stated or implied by the speakers you hear. Do not take notes or write in your test book at any time. Do not turn the pages until you are told to do so.

sections.title: STRUCTURE AND WRITTEN EXPRESSION

parts.title: Structure
parts.instruction:
This section is designed to measure your ability to recognize language that is appropriate for standard written English. There are two types of questions in this section, with special directions for each type.

Questions 1-15 are incomplete sentences. Beneath each sentence you will see four words or phrases, marked (A), (B), (C), and (D). Choose the one word or phrase that best completes the sentence. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. Fill in the space so that the letter inside the oval cannot be seen. 

Look at the following examples 
The president ____________ the election by a landslide.
(A) won 
(B) he won 
(C) yesterday 
(D) fortunately 

The sentence should read, "The president won the election by a landslide." Therefore, you should choose (A).

questions.question: The North Pole ___________ a latitude of 90 degrees north.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence because it adds a subject and verb ('it has') to an already complete clause ('The North Pole'), resulting in two independent clauses joined without proper punctuation or conjunction.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang berlebihan (run-on sentence) karena menambahkan subjek dan kata kerja ('it has') ke klausa yang sudah lengkap ('The North Pole'), menghasilkan dua klausa independen yang digabungkan tanpa tanda baca atau konjungsi yang tepat.

reason.en: This option uses a present progressive verb ('is having') which is typically used for actions happening at the moment of speaking or for temporary situations. The fact that the North Pole has a specific latitude is a permanent, factual statement, making the simple present tense more appropriate.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata kerja present progressive ('is having') yang biasanya digunakan untuk tindakan yang terjadi pada saat berbicara atau untuk situasi sementara. Fakta bahwa Kutub Utara memiliki garis lintang tertentu adalah pernyataan faktual yang permanen, sehingga bentuk simple present lebih tepat.

reason.en: This option creates a dependent clause ('which is having a latitude of 90 degrees north') that cannot stand alone as a complete sentence. It needs an independent clause to modify, but the sentence begins with 'The North Pole' which is already a subject.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan klausa dependen ('which is having a latitude of 90 degrees north') yang tidak dapat berdiri sendiri sebagai kalimat lengkap. Klausa ini memerlukan klausa independen untuk dimodifikasi, tetapi kalimat dimulai dengan 'The North Pole' yang sudah merupakan subjek.

reason.en: This option correctly completes the sentence by providing a main verb ('has') that agrees with the singular subject ('The North Pole'). The sentence now has a subject and a verb, forming a complete and grammatically correct statement.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara benar melengkapi kalimat dengan menyediakan kata kerja utama ('has') yang sesuai dengan subjek tunggal ('The North Pole'). Kalimat sekarang memiliki subjek dan kata kerja, membentuk pernyataan yang lengkap dan benar secara tata bahasa.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The city of Beverly Hills is surrounded on ___________ the city of Los Angeles.

reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang tidak lengkap secara tata bahasa. 'its sides' tidak berfungsi sebagai klausa lengkap atau frasa yang cocok untuk melengkapi struktur kalimat.

reason.en: This option is grammatically incorrect and does not fit the sentence structure. It introduces a subject and verb ('the sides are') in a place where a prepositional phrase or adverbial phrase is needed.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak lengkap secara tata bahasa dan tidak sesuai dengan struktur kalimat. Opsi ini memperkenalkan subjek dan kata kerja ('the sides are') di tempat yang membutuhkan frasa preposisional atau frasa adverbial.

reason.en: This option is grammatically incorrect and does not fit the sentence structure. 'it is the side of' is a clause that cannot function as a prepositional phrase.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak lengkap secara tata bahasa dan tidak sesuai dengan struktur kalimat. 'it is the side of' adalah klausa yang tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai frasa preposisional.

reason.en: This option correctly completes the sentence by providing the necessary prepositional phrase 'by all sides'. The structure 'surrounded on all sides by' is a common idiomatic expression indicating enclosure.
reason.id: Opsi ini melengkapi kalimat dengan benar dengan menyediakan frasa preposisional yang diperlukan 'by all sides'. Struktur 'surrounded on all sides by' adalah ungkapan idiomatik umum yang menunjukkan pengepungan.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 15: Subject/Verb Inversion

questions.question: ___________ greyhound, can achieve speeds up to thirty-six miles per hour.

reason.en: This option only provides an article ('The') and does not create a complete subject for the sentence. The sentence needs a subject that can be modified by the appositive phrase 'the fastest dog'.
reason.id: Opsi ini hanya menyediakan artikel ('The') dan tidak menciptakan subjek yang lengkap untuk kalimat tersebut. Kalimat membutuhkan subjek yang dapat dimodifikasi oleh frasa aposisi 'the fastest dog'.

reason.en: This option provides 'The fastest', which could function as a subject, but it lacks a noun to be modified. The phrase 'the fastest' needs to be followed by a noun (like 'dog') to form a complete subject that can be described by the subsequent appositive.
reason.id: Opsi ini menyediakan 'The fastest', yang dapat berfungsi sebagai subjek, tetapi tidak memiliki kata benda yang akan dimodifikasi. Frasa 'the fastest' perlu diikuti oleh kata benda (seperti 'dog') untuk membentuk subjek lengkap yang dapat dijelaskan oleh aposisi berikutnya.

reason.en: This option provides 'The fastest dog', which is a complete subject. However, it does not correctly set up the relationship with the following phrase 'the greyhound'. The structure requires a connector or a way to link these two noun phrases, which is achieved by using a comma after 'dog' to indicate an appositive.
reason.id: Opsi ini menyediakan 'The fastest dog', yang merupakan subjek lengkap. Namun, opsi ini tidak secara benar mengatur hubungan dengan frasa berikutnya 'the greyhound'. Struktur ini memerlukan konektor atau cara untuk menghubungkan kedua frasa kata benda ini, yang dicapai dengan menggunakan koma setelah 'dog' untuk menunjukkan aposisi.

reason.en: This option correctly forms a complete subject ('The fastest dog') and uses a comma to introduce an appositive phrase ('the greyhound') that renames the subject. This structure is common in English and allows the sentence to flow correctly, identifying the greyhound as the fastest dog.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara benar membentuk subjek lengkap ('The fastest dog') dan menggunakan koma untuk memperkenalkan frasa aposisi ('the greyhound') yang mengganti nama subjek. Struktur ini umum dalam bahasa Inggris dan memungkinkan kalimat mengalir dengan benar, mengidentifikasi greyhound sebagai anjing tercepat.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 12: Use Adjective Clause Connector/Subjects Correctly

questions.question: Marmots spend their time foraging among meadow plants and flowers or__________ on rocky cliffs.

reason.en: This option is a verb phrase ('gets sun') that does not fit the parallel structure with 'foraging'. 'Foraging' is a present participle acting as part of a verb phrase. 'Gets sun' is a simple present tense verb phrase.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah frasa kata kerja ('gets sun') yang tidak sesuai dengan struktur paralel dengan 'foraging'. 'Foraging' adalah present participle yang berfungsi sebagai bagian dari frasa kata kerja. 'Gets sun' adalah frasa kata kerja present tense.

reason.en: This option is a present participle ('sunning') that fits the parallel structure with 'foraging'. The sentence structure implies two parallel activities: 'Marmots spend their time foraging... or sunning...'. This requires parallel verb forms.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah present participle ('sunning') yang sesuai dengan struktur paralel dengan 'foraging'. Struktur kalimat menyiratkan dua aktivitas paralel: 'Marmots spend their time foraging... or sunning...'. Ini membutuhkan bentuk kata kerja yang paralel.

reason.en: This option is a noun phrase ('the sun') and cannot be parallel to the present participle 'foraging'.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah frasa kata benda ('the sun') dan tidak bisa paralel dengan present participle 'foraging'.

reason.en: This option is an adjective ('sunny') and cannot be parallel to the present participle 'foraging'.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah kata sifat ('sunny') dan tidak bisa paralel dengan present participle 'foraging'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 15: Subject/Verb Inversion

questions.question: The greenhouse effect occurs _______________ heat radiated from the Sun.

options.text: when does the Earth s atmosphere trap

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence because it includes a question word ('when') followed by a subject and verb, but it doesn't form a subordinate clause that can correctly follow 'occurs'. The structure 'when does the Earth's atmosphere trap' is interrogative and cannot function as a subordinate clause.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang berlarut-larut karena menyertakan kata tanya ('when') diikuti oleh subjek dan predikat, tetapi tidak membentuk klausa subordinat yang dapat mengikuti 'occurs' dengan benar. Struktur 'when does the Earth's atmosphere trap' bersifat interogatif dan tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai klausa subordinat.

reason.en: This option is an inverted question structure ('does the Earth's atmosphere trap') and cannot function as a subordinate clause following the main verb 'occurs'. The sentence needs a clause that explains *when* the greenhouse effect occurs.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah struktur pertanyaan terbalik ('does the Earth's atmosphere trap') dan tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai klausa subordinat setelah kata kerja utama 'occurs'. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan klausa yang menjelaskan *kapan* efek rumah kaca terjadi.

reason.en: This option correctly forms a subordinate adverbial clause of time/cause. 'When the Earth's atmosphere traps' has a subject ('Earth's atmosphere') and a verb ('traps') and functions as a subordinate clause explaining *when* the greenhouse effect occurs, fitting perfectly after 'occurs'. This aligns with Skill 7 which discusses using adverb connectors like 'when' to join clauses.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara benar membentuk klausa adverbial subordinat waktu/sebab. 'When the Earth's atmosphere traps' memiliki subjek ('Earth's atmosphere') dan kata kerja ('traps') dan berfungsi sebagai klausa subordinat yang menjelaskan *kapan* efek rumah kaca terjadi, sangat cocok setelah 'occurs'. Ini sesuai dengan Skill 7 yang membahas penggunaan konektor adverbia seperti 'when' untuk menggabungkan klausa.

reason.en: This option provides only a noun phrase ('the Earth's atmosphere traps') without a connector like 'when' or 'that'. Makna yang dimaksud kemungkinan adalah bahwa serigala masih hidup dan berkeliaran, atau bahwa serigala itu sendiri masih hidup. Penempatannya di sini canggung dan berpotensi salah, karena dapat menyiratkan bahwa program pemusnahan itu hidup. Frasa yang lebih standar adalah 'live wolves roamed' atau 'wolves, alive, roamed'. Ini adalah potensi kesalahan dalam penggunaan atau penempatan kata sifat.

reason.en: The phrase 'across nearly' functions correctly as an adverbial phrase indicating the extent of the roaming. It does not contain an error.
reason.id: Frasa 'across nearly' berfungsi dengan benar sebagai frasa keterangan yang menunjukkan sejauh mana pergerakan. Frasa ini tidak mengandung kesalahan.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 22: Listen for Who and What with Multiple Nouns

questions.question: ~[During]~(A) the 1960s the Berkeley campus of the University of California ~[came to]~(B) national attention as a ~[result]~(C) its radical ~[political]~(D) activity.

reason.en: The word 'During' is a preposition that correctly introduces a time phrase. It is grammatically sound in this position.
reason.id: Kata 'During' adalah preposisi yang memperkenalkan frasa waktu dengan benar. Kata ini secara tata bahasa benar pada posisi ini.

reason.en: The phrase 'came to' functions as a verb phrase, indicating the action of gaining attention. It is grammatically correct in this context.
reason.id: Frasa 'came to' berfungsi sebagai frasa kata kerja, yang menunjukkan tindakan mendapatkan perhatian. Frasa ini secara tata bahasa benar dalam konteks ini.

reason.en: The sentence structure requires a connector that shows cause and effect, such as 'because of' or 'as a result of'. 'Result' alone is a noun and does not function as a connector here. The phrase 'as a result of' is needed to connect the cause (political activity) to the effect (national attention). This relates to Skill 6 which covers correct use of connectors.
reason.id: Struktur kalimat memerlukan konektor yang menunjukkan sebab dan akibat, seperti 'because of' atau 'as a result of'. 'Result' saja adalah kata benda dan tidak berfungsi sebagai konektor di sini. Frasa 'as a result of' diperlukan untuk menghubungkan sebab (aktivitas politik) dengan akibat (perhatian nasional). Ini berkaitan dengan Skill 6 yang mencakup penggunaan konektor yang benar.

reason.en: The word 'political' is an adjective modifying the noun 'activity'. It is used correctly in this position.
reason.id: Kata 'political' adalah kata sifat yang memodifikasi kata benda 'activity'. Kata ini digunakan dengan benar pada posisi ini.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 6: Use Coordinate Connectors Correctly

questions.question: ~[Artist Gutzon]~(A) Borglum designed the Mount Rushmore Memorial and worked on ~[project]~(B) from 1925 ~[until]~(C) ~[his]~(D) death in 1941.

reason.en: The name 'Artist Gutzon Borglum' is a proper noun and functions correctly as the subject of the sentence. It is not an error.
reason.id: Nama 'Artist Gutzon Borglum' adalah kata benda proper dan berfungsi dengan benar sebagai subjek kalimat. Ini bukan kesalahan.

reason.en: The word 'project' is not needed here. The phrase 'worked on from 1925 until his death' already modifies the subject 'Artist Gutzon Borglum' and describes his work on the memorial. Adding 'project' creates an unnecessary and awkward phrase, making it the error.
reason.id: Kata 'project' tidak diperlukan di sini. Frasa 'worked on from 1925 until his death' sudah memodifikasi subjek 'Artist Gutzon Borglum' dan menjelaskan pekerjaannya pada monumen tersebut. Penambahan 'project' menciptakan frasa yang tidak perlu dan canggung, menjadikannya kesalahan.

reason.en: The word 'until' correctly indicates the end point of the action (working on the memorial) and is grammatically sound in this context.
reason.id: Kata 'until' secara akurat menunjukkan titik akhir dari tindakan (mengerjakan monumen) dan secara tata bahasa benar dalam konteks ini.

reason.en: The possessive pronoun 'his' correctly refers to Gutzon Borglum and is necessary to indicate whose death marked the end of his work.
reason.id: Kata ganti posesif 'his' secara akurat merujuk pada Gutzon Borglum dan diperlukan untuk menunjukkan kematian siapa yang menandai akhir karyanya.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 3: Be Careful of Appositives

questions.question: It is proving less ~[costly]~(A) and more ~[profitably]~(B) for drugmakers ~[to market]~(C) ~[directly]~(D) to patients.

reason.en: The sentence structure requires an adjective to modify the noun 'drugmakers'. 'Costly' is an adjective.
reason.id: Struktur kalimat membutuhkan kata sifat untuk memodifikasi kata benda 'drugmakers'. 'Costly' adalah kata sifat.

reason.en: The sentence requires an adjective to modify the noun 'drugmakers'. 'Profitably' is an adverb and modifies verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs, not nouns.
reason.id: Kalimat membutuhkan kata sifat untuk memodifikasi kata benda 'drugmakers'. 'Profitably' adalah kata keterangan dan memodifikasi kata kerja, kata sifat, atau kata keterangan lainnya, bukan kata benda.

reason.en: The phrase 'to market' is grammatically correct in this context, functioning as an infinitive indicating purpose or action.
reason.id: Frasa 'to market' secara tata bahasa benar dalam konteks ini, berfungsi sebagai infinitif yang menunjukkan tujuan atau tindakan.

reason.en: The word 'directly' is an adverb modifying the infinitive 'to market', which is grammatically correct.
reason.id: Kata 'directly' adalah kata keterangan yang memodifikasi infinitif 'to market', yang secara tata bahasa benar.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 46: Adjectives and Adverbs

questions.question: Sapphires ~[weighing]~(A) ~[as]~(B) much as ~[two pounds]~(C) have on occasion ~[mined]~(D).

reason.en: The word 'weighing' is a present participle. In this sentence structure, a past participle is needed to form the passive voice, indicating that the sapphires were mined.
reason.id: Kata 'weighing' adalah present participle. Dalam struktur kalimat ini, past participle diperlukan untuk membentuk passive voice, yang menunjukkan bahwa safir tersebut ditambang.

reason.en: The word 'as' is used here to form a comparison of equality ('as much as'). While grammatically correct in a comparative structure, the sentence requires a past participle to complete the passive verb phrase.
reason.id: Kata 'as' digunakan di sini untuk membentuk perbandingan kesetaraan ('as much as'). Meskipun secara tata bahasa benar dalam struktur komparatif, kalimat ini memerlukan past participle untuk melengkapi frasa kata kerja pasif.

reason.en: 'Two pounds' is a noun phrase indicating weight. It is part of the comparative structure and does not represent the grammatical error in the sentence.
reason.id: 'Two pounds' adalah frasa nomina yang menunjukkan berat. Ini adalah bagian dari struktur komparatif dan tidak mewakili kesalahan tata bahasa dalam kalimat.

reason.en: The sentence requires a past participle to form the passive voice ('have been mined' or implied 'have mined' if the subject were the miner). Since sapphires are mined, the passive form is correct. 'Mined' is the past participle needed here.
reason.id: Kalimat ini memerlukan past participle untuk membentuk passive voice ('have been mined' atau tersirat 'have mined' jika subjeknya adalah penambang). Karena safir ditambang, bentuk pasif sudah benar. 'Mined' adalah past participle yang dibutuhkan di sini.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 30: Use of the Verb (Tense)

questions.question: ~[Like]~(A) snakes, lizards can ~[be found]~(B) on all ~[others]~(C) continents ~[except]~(D) Antarctica.

reason.en: The word 'Like' is used correctly here as a preposition to draw a comparison between snakes and lizards.
reason.id: Kata 'Like' digunakan dengan benar di sini sebagai preposisi untuk menarik perbandingan antara ular dan kadal.

reason.en: The phrase 'can be found' is a correct passive verb construction, indicating that lizards are the object of the action.
reason.id: Frasa 'can be found' adalah konstruksi kata kerja pasif yang benar, menunjukkan bahwa kadal adalah objek dari tindakan tersebut.

reason.en: The word 'others' is a pronoun and is used incorrectly here. The sentence needs an adjective to modify 'continents'. The correct word should be 'other'. 'Others' refers to other people or things, not other continents.
reason.id: Kata 'others' adalah kata ganti (pronoun) dan digunakan secara tidak benar di sini. Kalimat ini membutuhkan kata sifat untuk memodifikasi 'continents'. Kata yang benar seharusnya adalah 'other'. 'Others' merujuk pada orang atau benda lain, bukan benua lain.

reason.en: The word 'except' is used correctly as a preposition to indicate exclusion.
reason.id: Kata 'except' digunakan dengan benar sebagai preposisi untuk menunjukkan pengecualian.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 58: Commonly Confused Words

questions.question: Banks, savings and loans, and finance companies ~[have recently]~(A) been ~[doing]~(B) home equity loans with ~[greater frequency]~(C) than ~[ever before]~(D).

reason.en: This is a correct adverb of time, modifying the verb phrase.
reason.id: Ini adalah adverbia waktu yang benar, memodifikasi frasa kata kerja.

reason.en: The verb 'doing' is incorrect here. The sentence requires the present perfect tense ('have done') to indicate an action that started in the past and continues to the present or has a result in the present. 'Doing' is the present participle and needs a form of 'be' to form a continuous tense, or 'have' to form a perfect tense. In this context, the present perfect is appropriate.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'doing' di sini salah. Kalimat ini membutuhkan present perfect tense ('have done') untuk menunjukkan tindakan yang dimulai di masa lalu dan berlanjut hingga sekarang atau memiliki hasil di masa sekarang. 'Doing' adalah present participle dan membutuhkan bentuk 'be' untuk membentuk continuous tense, atau 'have' untuk membentuk perfect tense. Dalam konteks ini, present perfect sudah tepat.

reason.en: This is a correct comparative phrase, indicating a higher degree of frequency.
reason.id: Ini adalah frasa komparatif yang benar, menunjukkan tingkat frekuensi yang lebih tinggi.

reason.en: This is a correct adverbial phrase indicating a point in time.
reason.id: Ini adalah frasa adverbial yang benar yang menunjukkan titik waktu.
